description.value:
Source: Ariadne's Book of Legends - Alpha v1.2, p. 54-57.

Level 20

Legendary Points

Starting at 20th level, you gain Legendary Points. Your level determines the number of points you have, as shown in the Legendary Points column of the Legendary Paladin table. You can take the Recover action on your turn to regain all expended Legendary Points. You can spend these points to fuel various features.

Legendary Resistance

Starting at 20th level, you can expend 1 Legendary Point when you fail a saving throw to succeed instead.

Legendary AC

At 20th level, if your AC calculation is lower than your level, you can use your level as your AC instead. If you are using your Legendary AC, you cannot further increase your AC in any way, except by temporary AC increases or magical effects lasting 1 hour or less. Other spells and effects that increase your AC only affect your regular AC.

Paladin’s AC. Increase your AC by 3. This bonus is increased by 1 when you reach 22nd and 24th level.

Legendary Smite

Also at 20th level, you learn to channel a singular oathborn strike. You can use your action and expend 1 Legendary Point to perform a Legendary Smite. Make a

— Spell Slots Per Spell Level

Prepared

— Points 1st 2nd 3rd 4th 5th 6th 7th

Divinity

Spells

1 4 16 4 3 3 3 2 1 —

2 4 17 4 3 3 3 2 1 1

3 4 17 4 3 3 3 2 1 1

single melee weapon attack against a creature within range. For this attack, the reach of your weapon is increased to 30 feet.

On a hit, the attack deals radiant damage rather than your weapon’s normal damage type. This attack does not benefit from any other bonus to its damage rolls. You can expend a spell slot alongside the Legendary Point to increase the damage of the strike, at a rate of 1d8 per spell slot level expended, as shown below.

Legendary Smite Damage

Spell Slot Expended Damage

None 3d8 x 5

1st level 4d8 x 5

2nd level 5d8 x 5

3rd level 6d8 x 5

4th level 7d8 x 5

5th level 8d8 x 5

6th level 9d8 x 5

7th level 10d8 x 5

When you score a critical hit with your Legendary Smite, you do not multiply the damage dice by 2, but instead add 2d8 to the equation.

source.custom: Original en-US text from Alpha v1.2
